en: Do you know that using very is actually spoiling your English and stopping you
from speaking professionally? Well, instead of using very too many times
before an adjective, you can use a different vocabulary. So, for example, very
afraid, fearful, very angry, furious, very accurate, exact, very bad, awful. Let's look
at some more examples and this time use them in sentences. So, sentence number one
the weather is very cold. Instead of saying very cold, you can say the weather
is freezing. Another example can be this place is very calm. Instead of saying
very calm, you can say this place is serene. So, next time when you speak too
many very's in your sentences, try to use an alternate vocabulary and see how
